steps.title: Find the starting value
steps.content: The problem gives $q(0)=18$, so the exponential model must start with coefficient $18$.

steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content:
A $35\%$ decrease means each new value keeps $100\%-35\%=65\%$ of the previous value. The decay factor is $0.65$, so:
$$q(x)=18(0.65)^x$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C — $q \left(x\right)=18{ \left(0.65\right)}^{x}$ is the only option with initial value $18$ and decay factor $0.65$. Choice A — $q \left(x\right)=0.65{ \left(18\right)}^{x}$ swaps the coefficient and base. Choice B — $q \left(x\right)=1.35{ \left(18\right)}^{x}$ and Choice D — $q \left(x\right)=18{ \left(1.35\right)}^{x}$ use growth factors instead of the decay factor $0.65$.
